philosophy: tafagent as integrator, not silo. For each documented LLM-eval pain we surface: (a) the tafagent mode that addresses it, if any; (b) the best-of-breed external tools the community already trusts; (c) when to use which. Goal: complete coverage, not feature lock-in. If the canonical tool exists elsewhere we link, not rebuild.
verification_note: All external URLs were fetched and confirmed alive on the compiled date. Treat older entries with skepticism — link rot is real. Report dead links via the GitHub issue tracker.

diagnostic.label: Diagnose a model
diagnostic.description: Will this model work for my use case?

training.label: Train / fine-tune safely
training.description: Don't waste GPU time on broken setups.

observability.label: Observe & debug agents
observability.description: What is my agent actually doing?

entries.pain: Benchmark saturation — top models all tied at 90%+, score no longer informative.
entries.best_for: Quick check whether MMLU / AIME / HumanEval still discriminate frontier models in 2026.
entries.not_for: Predicting which model will win on a non-standard benchmark.

entries.pain: Benchmark contamination — model trained on the test set.
entries.best_for: Estimating contamination probability across 20+ public benchmarks per architecture.
entries.not_for: Definitive proof — needs trace inspection. Treat as prior, not certainty.

entries.pain: Vendor-reported scores untrustworthy (Llama 4 mixed-quality reports).
entries.best_for: Cross-checking vendor blog claims against community-verified runs before quoting.
entries.not_for: Models that have never been independently verified — assume vendor optimism.

entries.pain: Chatbot Arena hides confidence intervals — many top-Elo wins are statistically tied.
entries.best_for: Reconstructing 95% CIs from raw vote CSVs to flag statistical ties.
entries.not_for: Inferring true skill — Arena measures preference, not capability.

entries.pain: Same model, different scores on different setups — bug or noise?

external_tools.name: vLLM vs HF transformers consistency study

entries.best_for: Predicting maximum admissible numerical gap between two evaluation frameworks.
entries.not_for: Identifying the exact root cause — narrows down candidates only.

entries.pain: Models can strategically underperform on capability evaluations.
entries.best_for: Awareness — knowing CoT monitoring can have up to 36% false-negative rate.
entries.not_for: Live detection — requires running the model and adversarial probes.

entries.pain: Config claims 32k/128k context but model attends way less (SWA, YaRN).
entries.best_for: 1-second verdict (HONEST / INFLATED / SEVERELY INFLATED / YARN-EXTENDED) before paying GPU.
entries.not_for: Validating that the model reasons (vs. just retrieves) at the effective context — use NIAH→Reason.

entries.pain: Long-context models pass NIAH but fail multi-hop reasoning.
entries.best_for: Predicting NIAH and reasoning pass rates from architecture alone — no inference needed.
entries.not_for: Final go/no-go decision — re-test on your domain after architectural screening passes.

entries.pain: Long-ctx accuracy drops well before the claimed 128K window — but raw scores are dominated by base ability and hide which model truly retains capability past short context.
entries.best_for: Picking a long-ctx model by relative degradation rather than raw score. Uses peer-reviewed LongScore metric to disentangle base ability from long-ctx capability. Browser-only, no GPU.
entries.not_for: Models not yet covered by RULER or HELMET (KB has n=93). Use the external tools above for breadth.

entries.pain: Glitch tokens / merge residues break inference silently.
entries.best_for: Spotting weird tokens. ~4.3% of vocab in Llama-2 / Mistral / DeepSeek-V3 are glitches.
entries.not_for: Fixing them — requires finetuning or vocab patching.

entries.pain: Where does my model sit in the architecture phase space (γ × θ)?
entries.best_for: Visualizing 23 reference models and locating yours by Hagedorn line / Padé curve.
entries.not_for: Quantitative recipe scoring — use Profile mode instead.

entries.pain: Will this model fit my use case across all 5 recipes?
entries.best_for: Scoring all 5 recipes (custom train vs API · long context · budget · hardware · KV cache · etc.) in one pass.
entries.not_for: Production deployment readiness — Profile is screening, not certification.

entries.pain: Forgetting `--apply_chat_template` silently halves multi-turn accuracy.
entries.best_for: Detecting which family (Llama-3 / ChatML / Mistral / Gemma / Phi-3 / DeepSeek / Alpaca) and getting the exact CLI flag.
entries.not_for: Custom templates outside the 7 detected families — verify manually.

entries.pain: JSON schema engines fail silently; CoT models commit to answer before reasoning.
entries.best_for: Constrained decoding for production. Use llguidance / Outlines / SGLang grammars for 100% schema-valid output.
entries.not_for: Quick prototypes — function calling is sufficient (95-99% reliable).

entries.pain: MCP server schema doesn't conform to spec — clients silently break.
entries.best_for: One-shot validation of tool/resource/prompt schemas before publishing an MCP server.
entries.not_for: Runtime testing — use the official inspector for live calls.

entries.pain: Need to measure γ_obs on real weights, not just predict from config.
entries.best_for: Generating the exact `python cli/diagnose_model.py` command for your model.
entries.not_for: Browser-only diagnosis — this mode is a builder, not an executor.

entries.pain: Tokenizers tax non-English text asymmetrically — Chinese/Arabic/Hindi can be 2-3× the token count of English on Latin-trained tokenizers (Llama, Phi, GPT-4 cl100k), silently 3×ing API bills and halving effective context.

external_tools.name: OpenAI cookbook — counting tokens with tiktoken

entries.best_for: Validating multilingual API cost projections before scaling. Compare REAL per-tokenizer counts on production-representative text across Qwen / Phi / Llama / Gemma / GPT-4 / Claude in one click — surfaces the worst offender for your specific text mix (e.g. Phi-3.5 = 2.27× Qwen2.5 on Chinese paragraphs).
entries.not_for: Tokenizer vocab compatibility for speculative decoding — that's a different question, use 🔬 Spec-Decode instead.

entries.pain: Speculative decoding silently rejects all draft tokens when target+draft tokenizer vocabs differ — worse throughput than baseline, no error.
entries.best_for: Verifying a (target, draft) pair before launching a vLLM/SGLang cluster with spec-dec enabled. Surfaces vocab-size, type, and special-token mismatches.
entries.not_for: Measuring real-world acceptance rate α — that requires running both models on your domain. The tool only predicts a band based on param ratio.

entries.pain: `get_peft_model()` before `PeftModel.from_pretrained()` silently loads base model — LoRA weights ignored.
entries.best_for: If you suspect your LoRA isn't being applied, call `model.get_layer_status()` and check `active_adapters` is non-empty.

entries.pain: LoRA introduces 'intruder dimensions' that contribute to forgetting.
entries.best_for: Post-training cleanup if forgetting metrics regress after LoRA finetune.
entries.not_for: Heavy domain shift — intruder dim removal won't fix structural forgetting.

entries.pain: Will quantization break my model? Which scheme for which arch?
entries.best_for: Predict γ shift + ΔPPL for any (model × scheme) combo. AWQ ~95% / GGUF ~92% / GPTQ ~90% retention.
entries.not_for: Production quality cert — run a 10-prompt holdout eval after quantization.

entries.pain: Will my LoRA fine-tune destroy MMLU performance?

external_tools.name: Scaling Laws for Forgetting (Kleiman et al.)
LoRA Learns Less and Forgets Less (Biderman et al., TMLR)
How Much is Too Much? (LoRA Rank Trade-offs)

entries.best_for: Reading before any new fine-tune. Same (arch, rank) yields Δ from -10pp to +35pp on MMLU.
entries.not_for: A predictor — variance is too high for a closed-form heuristic. Measure your own holdout.

entries.pain: Is my RAG retrieval actually retrieving?
entries.best_for: Production RAG monitoring. RAGAS for metric exploration, DeepEval for CI/CD gates, TruLens for dashboards.
entries.not_for: Browser-only — all three need Python + your retrieval pipeline.

entries.pain: Which embedding model for my corpus?
entries.best_for: Cross-comparison of 100+ embedding models on 56 English tasks / 250+ multilingual.
entries.not_for: Predicting performance on your specific corpus — 'leaderboard ≠ your data'.

entries.pain: Which VLM benchmark, and is my VLM actually seeing?
entries.best_for: MMMU near-saturated (top-3 ~85.6%); VisScience still discriminative (~46% mean) — pick the harder one.
entries.not_for: Visual hallucination detection — needs running the VLM with your images.

entries.pain: Why did my agent fail / loop? Can't tell from logs.
entries.best_for: Visual trace viewer per LLM call / tool invocation / retrieval step. Token + cost tracking.
entries.not_for: Browser-only — all need integration into your stack.

entries.pain: Best agentic models follow <30% of instructions perfectly on real-world tasks.
entries.best_for: Calibrating expectations — performance falls with instruction length and tool constraints.
entries.not_for: Live testing — needs running the agent on your task suite.

entries.pain: I want to read the full state of LLM evaluation 2026.

external_tools.name: Survey: A Survey on LLM Benchmarks (2508.15361)

entries.best_for: Comprehensive context on contamination, judge bias, saturation, methodology open problems.
entries.not_for: Quick decisions — these are surveys, not tools.

entries.pain: What's actually in this model's config.json?

external_tools.name: HF Hub model card / config viewer

entries.best_for: Paste config JSON → full TAF analysis without re-fetching.
entries.not_for: Comparing across N models — use 🆚 Compare or open-llm-leaderboard/comparator.

entries.pain: Side-by-side comparison of multiple models on multiple recipes.
entries.best_for: Quick recipe-by-recipe comparison up to 5 models.
entries.not_for: Production benchmark scores — use the HF comparator for benchmark results.

entries.pain: I just want to ask a question in plain English.
entries.best_for: 'Will Mistral-7B handle 16K NIAH retrieval?' → answer with the right recipe + chain.
entries.not_for: Open-ended chat — this is a routing front-end, not a chatbot.

entries.pain: I know my use case but not which recipe to apply.
entries.best_for: Browsing the 8 recipes (custom train vs API · long context · budget · hardware · etc.) when you don't know which fits.
entries.not_for: Running all of them at once — use Profile mode.

entries.pain: Can I trust the math behind the diagnostic?
entries.best_for: 37 theorems machine-proven in Lean 4 + Mathlib. Click any badge in the UI to open the source line.
entries.not_for: Empirical claims — Lean covers algebraic identities, not measurement protocols.
